 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.1.3","title":"Task: Terminal compatibility + risk register (inline mode, Windows quirks, headless CI)","description":"## Why
TUIs fail in subtle ways: raw mode leaks, alternate screen not restored, resize storms, missing key chords, Windows console oddities. We need an explicit risk register with mitigation tasks so the rewrite is not death-by-edge-case.

## Deliverables
- Compatibility matrix of behaviors across:
  - Linux terminals (xterm, kitty, alacritty)
  - macOS terminals
  - Windows Terminal / ConPTY
- Risk register entries (each with mitigation + test coverage):
  - raw mode cleanup on panic/ctrl-c
  - inline mode remeasure + scrollback preservation
  - resize storms and event coalescing
  - mouse capture differences
  - clipboard support / fallbacks
  - performance on huge result lists
  - headless mode semantics used by CI tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every risk has:
  - probability/impact estimate
  - concrete mitigation
  - at least one planned automated test or harness hook","notes":"## Terminal compatibility matrix (ftui migration risk baseline)

Legend: ✅ expected; ⚠ partial/quirky; ❌ unsupported; N/A not applicable.

### Behavior coverage by platform family

| Behavior / concern | Linux terminals (xterm/kitty/alacritty) | macOS terminals (Terminal.app/iTerm2/WezTerm) | Windows Terminal + ConPTY |
|---|---|---|---|
| Raw mode enter/exit stability | ✅ stable with crossterm + RAII guard | ✅ stable, but ensure SIGINT/SIGTERM cleanup path | ⚠ mostly stable; cleanup on abnormal termination can be inconsistent |
| Alternate screen lifecycle | ✅ reliable; preserves primary scrollback on exit | ✅ reliable in iTerm2/WezTerm; Terminal.app occasionally redraw-jumps | ⚠ supported, but restore artifacts can occur under ConPTY + rapid resize |
| Inline mode (no alt-screen) viewport anchoring | ✅ good if explicit remeasure + redraw throttling | ⚠ good in iTerm2/WezTerm; Terminal.app line-wrap edge cases | ⚠ works but scroll region/viewport math differs and needs explicit ConPTY tuning |
| Resize event behavior under storms | ⚠ bursty events; requires coalescing/debounce | ⚠ bursty during pane split/zoom; requires debounce | ❌ worst-case storm behavior; mandatory coalescing + frame cap |
| Mouse capture + wheel semantics | ✅ mostly uniform | ⚠ event encoding differences (especially Terminal.app) | ⚠ button/wheel mapping differs; drag reporting inconsistent |
| Clipboard integration | ⚠ OSC52 varies by emulator policy | ⚠ iTerm2/WezTerm strong; Terminal.app limited pathways | ⚠ native clipboard APIs available but terminal passthrough inconsistent |
| Key chord fidelity (Ctrl/Alt/Meta/F-keys) | ✅ generally reliable | ⚠ Option/Meta translation differs by profile | ⚠ AltGr + Ctrl combos differ; some chords swallowed by host shell |
| Unicode width + grapheme handling | ⚠ depends on wcwidth/table version | ⚠ emoji/ZWJ can differ by font + renderer | ⚠ widest variance; requires strict width fallback + truncation guards |
| High-volume list rendering performance | ✅ good with diff renderer + virtualization | ✅ good with virtualization; avoid full repaint | ⚠ acceptable with virtualization; full repaint regresses quickly |
| Headless CI semantics (`TUI_HEADLESS=1`, `--once`) | ✅ deterministic if no raw-mode path | ✅ deterministic if no terminal probing side effects | ✅ deterministic when forced fallback path bypasses terminal APIs |

### Specific terminal classes to test explicitly

| Terminal class | Known strength | Known hazard | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| Linux: xterm | canonical escape baseline | older xterm variants expose limited mouse features | High |
| Linux: kitty | modern key/mouse features | custom keyboard protocol interactions | Medium |
| Linux: alacritty | fast rendering | strict escape handling can expose bugs | High |
| macOS: Terminal.app | default user baseline | weaker mouse/inline behavior, Option-key ambiguity | High |
| macOS: iTerm2 | rich feature support | profile-dependent Meta/Option mapping | High |
| macOS: WezTerm | modern + performant | rapid resize churn in split panes | Medium |
| Windows: Terminal (ConPTY) | official modern path | resize + alt-screen cleanup edge cases | Critical |

## Risk register with mitigation and planned automated coverage

### R1: Raw mode cleanup on panic / ctrl-c / abnormal exit
- Probability: Medium (35%)
- Impact: Critical (terminal left unusable until `reset`)
- Mitigation:
  - Wrap terminal lifecycle in RAII session guard with `Drop` restore.
  - Add signal-aware shutdown path (`Ctrl+C`, termination) that reuses same restore routine.
  - Keep a best-effort emergency restore in panic hook for interactive mode only.
- Planned automated coverage:
  - PTY integration test: force panic inside event loop and assert cooked mode restored.
  - PTY integration test: send SIGINT and assert alternate screen/raw mode cleanup.
  - Regression harness: repeated enter/exit loop (N>=100) with no leaked terminal state.

### R2: Inline mode remeasure + scrollback preservation failures
- Probability: Medium-High (45%)
- Impact: High (layout drift, broken prompt/scrollback UX)
- Mitigation:
  - Recompute viewport measurements on every resize + prompt-line mutation.
  - Add explicit anchoring contract (status/input/footer rows) with invariant checks.
  - Fallback to full redraw when anchor invariants fail.
- Planned automated coverage:
  - Snapshot harness suite for inline mode at multiple terminal heights/widths.
  - PTY test with simulated wrapped lines before launch; verify scrollback preserved after exit.
  - Property-like test for measure->render idempotence across randomized dimensions.

### R3: Resize storms and event coalescing defects
- Probability: High (60%)
- Impact: High (jank, CPU spikes, stale layout)
- Mitigation:
  - Coalesce consecutive resize events within bounded debounce window.
  - Enforce max frame rate (e.g., 60fps cap) and skip redundant relayout frames.
  - Track last applied geometry hash to avoid duplicate renders.
- Planned automated coverage:
  - Synthetic event-stream test: 500 resize events burst, assert bounded render count.
  - Perf harness: budget assertion for CPU/render time under resize storm.
  - Trace-based test validating coalescing decisions in ftui runtime logs.

### R4: Mouse capture differences across emulators
- Probability: Medium (40%)
- Impact: Medium-High (selection/scroll UX regressions)
- Mitigation:
  - Normalize mouse events into internal canonical model (buttons/wheel/drag).
  - Feature-detect unavailable mouse modes and degrade to keyboard-first navigation.
  - Keep toggle to disable mouse capture when terminal reports malformed sequences.
- Planned automated coverage:
  - Unit tests for mouse event normalization mappings.
  - PTY fixture replay tests with captured escape sequences from Linux/macOS/Windows.
  - Integration smoke: enable/disable mouse capture and verify no crash + usable navigation.

### R5: Clipboard support and fallback behavior
- Probability: Medium (30%)
- Impact: Medium (copy UX inconsistent; no data loss risk)
- Mitigation:
  - Use layered strategy: native clipboard helper -> OSC52 -> user-facing fallback message.
  - Explicit capability probe + cached support flags per session.
  - Ensure failed clipboard writes are non-fatal and discoverable.
- Planned automated coverage:
  - Unit tests for clipboard strategy selection by capability matrix.
  - Integration tests with mocked clipboard backend failures.
  - Robot-mode test ensuring diagnostics stay on stderr, structured output unaffected.

### R6: Performance degradation on huge result lists
- Probability: High (55%)
- Impact: High (typing latency and frame drops)
- Mitigation:
  - Virtualized list rendering + bounded diff regions.
  - Incremental search/update pipeline with cancellation of stale queries.
  - Hard performance budgets for key path (input-to-first-frame latency, steady-state frame time).
- Planned automated coverage:
  - Benchmark fixture with >=50k results measuring p50/p95 render/input latency.
  - Regression benchmark gate in CI comparing against baseline budget envelope.
  - Profiling harness capturing diff size + render duration per frame.

### R7: Headless mode semantic drift (CI contract break)
- Probability: Medium (35%)
- Impact: Critical (CI flakes/failures, broken automation)
- Mitigation:
  - Keep explicit runtime selection path for `TUI_HEADLESS=1` and `--once`.
  - Prohibit terminal API calls in headless path; enforce with guard assertions.
  - Maintain deterministic output + exit codes identical to robot-mode expectations.
- Planned automated coverage:
  - Integration tests for all combinations: `{headless,on/off} x {once,on/off}`.
  - Contract tests for stdout/stderr separation and deterministic JSON outputs.
  - Negative test ensuring accidental terminal init in headless path fails test.

## Cross-risk execution order (mitigation sequencing)
1. Implement terminal session RAII + signal-safe cleanup (unblocks R1, reduces blast radius).
2. Add resize coalescing + geometry hash + frame budget instrumentation (R3, R6).
3. Land headless contract tests and guard rails before additional UI complexity (R7).
4. Roll in inline mode anchoring tests + terminal-specific PTY fixtures (R2, R4, R5).

## Proposed automated test matrix (minimum viable coverage)
- Unit tests:
  - event normalization (keyboard/mouse/resize)
  - inline measure/layout invariants
  - clipboard strategy routing
- Harness/snapshot:
  - golden snapshots across screen modes and color profiles
  - trace replay for resize storms and input bursts
- PTY integration:
  - lifecycle cleanup (panic/sigint)
  - keyboard/mouse smoke across representative terminals
  - scrollback preservation in inline mode
- CI headless:
  - deterministic `--once` behavior
  - no terminal API path in headless runtime","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:24:09.172248747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:29:21.221566516Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:29:21.221523766Z","close_reason":"Completed terminal compatibility matrix + quantified risk register with mitigation/tests in notes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","risk","spec","tui","windows"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.1.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:24:09.172248747Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.1.4","title":"Task: FrankenTUI deep-dive and mapping to cass TUI architecture","description":"## Why
We must constrain design to real ftui APIs and leverage built-in capabilities instead of re-implementing them.

## Deliverables
- A concrete mapping table (embedded in this bead) covering:
  - Terminal lifecycle: Ratatui+crossterm raw/alt-screen ↔ `ftui_core::TerminalSession` + `SessionOptions`
  - Screen modes: fullscreen vs `ScreenMode::Inline { ui_height }` and `UiAnchor`
  - Rendering pipeline: Ratatui Frame ↔ `ftui_render::Frame` + `BufferDiff`/Presenter
  - Event model: crossterm events ↔ `ftui_core::event::Event` and message conversion strategy
  - Layout system: Ratatui Layout ↔ `ftui-layout` (flex/grid) + intrinsic sizing (`ftui-widgets::measurable`)
  - Widgets: identify where we can directly use `ftui-widgets` (command palette, virtualized lists, modal, toast, help registry, json_view, log_viewer, scrollbar, table)
  - Runtime: current imperative loop ↔ `ftui_runtime::{Program, Model, Cmd, Subscription}`
  - Testing hooks: Ratatui TestBackend ↔ `ftui-harness` (snapshots, determinism, flicker detection, time travel)

## Key Decisions to Make (and record)
- Whether cass TUI uses:
  - a single `Model` with internal sub-state vs multiple screens/models
  - ftui focus graph (`ftui-widgets::focus`) for pane focus traversal
  - ftui reactive bindings (`ftui-runtime::reactive`) for inputs
- Whether we adopt ftui built-ins for:
  - command palette, help strip, toast/notification queue
  - virtualized results list (strongly preferred)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Documented mapping is detailed enough that implementation tasks can reference it directly.
- Any gaps in ftui are identified with an explicit plan:
  - implement missing widget in cass (only if truly cass-specific)
  - OR upstream to frankentui (preferred when generally useful)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:24:09.642963347Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:36:14.887866357Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T17:36:14.887839136Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","spec","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.1.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:24:09.642963347Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.1.5","title":"Task: Finalize interaction model + keymap for ftui runtime (focus, modals, degraded terminals)","description":"## Why
ftui brings a different runtime model (Elm/Bubbletea). We need a crisp, conflict-free, *discoverable* keymap and focus model that works across terminals and remains stable over time.

## Scope
- Start from `docs/tui_keymap_rfc.md` and current behavior in `src/ui/tui.rs`.
- Define:
  - global keys, pane focus traversal, modal stack semantics
  - action system contract (what actions exist, what state they require)
  - degraded key support fallbacks (function key absence, clipboard absence)
  - mouse optional behavior (hit regions)

## Deliverables
- Updated, self-contained keymap spec embedded in this bead:
  - includes all keys currently in `src/ui/shortcuts.rs`
  - documents any intentional changes ("breaking" is OK, but must be explicit)
- Focus model:
  - which pane has focus
  - how Tab/Shift+Tab moves focus
  - how overlays steal/restore focus
  - how inline searches (`/`) behave in results/detail

## Acceptance Criteria
- No conflicts: each key chord has one meaning per context.
- ESC safety: always backs out (clears in-pane search, closes modal, cancels prompt).
- Robot/headless clarity: what is disabled when `TUI_HEADLESS=1`.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:24:30.980731962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:39:23.355799938Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T17:39:23.355770053Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["a11y","ftui","spec","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.1.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:24:30.980731962Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.1","title":"Task: Add FrankenTUI (ftui) dependency + decide versioning strategy (git tag, features, CI)","description":"## Why
Before we can replace Ratatui, cass must compile and ship with the ftui stack in a reproducible way.

## What
1. Add ftui dependency to Cargo.toml: `ftui = { path = '/data/projects/frankentui/crates/ftui' }` with wildcard version
2. Add individual ftui-* crate deps as needed: ftui-extras with features (markdown, syntax, charts, canvas, theme, clipboard, export, visual-fx, forms, validation, help)
3. Decide versioning strategy: git tag vs path dependency vs published crate, CI pinning
4. Create a thin adapter module (`src/ui/ftui_adapter.rs`) that re-exports the ftui types we'll use most: Widget, StatefulWidget, Frame, Rect, Style, Model, Cmd, Program, etc. — gives us one import path to change later
5. Verify `cargo check --all-targets` passes with BOTH ratatui AND ftui present simultaneously (both must coexist during transition)

## Implementation Notes
- IMPORTANT: Do NOT remove ratatui yet — both must coexist during transition
- CI must build and test with ftui present but not yet wired to the main TUI
- ftui-extras features are additive — enable only what we need to minimize compile time
- Adapter shim makes future refactoring easier by centralizing ftui imports

## Tests
- `cargo check --all-targets` passes with zero errors
- `cargo clippy --all-targets` passes with zero warnings
- CI builds succeed with both ratatui and ftui in dependency tree
- Adapter module compiles and re-exports are accessible

## Acceptance Criteria
- Cargo.toml has ftui + ftui-extras with correct feature flags
- Both ratatui and ftui compile together without conflicts
- Adapter module exists with re-exports
- CI green","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:25:22.496435266Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:33:36.736268987Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T17:33:36.736234924Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","ftui","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:25:22.496435266Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.2","title":"Task: Define ftui-based TUI module boundaries (Model/Msg/Cmd, services, side effects)","description":"## Why
The current src/ui/tui.rs is an imperative, monolithic Ratatui loop. ftui's runtime encourages clear separation: pure update logic (state transition) vs side effects (Cmd). We need to define the boundaries before coding.

## What
Define the Elm architecture types for cass TUI:

### CassApp Model (implements ftui_runtime::Model)
Port ALL current state fields from tui.rs:
- query, results, panes (Vec<AgentPane>)
- filters (agents/workspaces/time/source)
- active_pane, pane_scroll_offset, detail_scroll
- theme selection (6 themes: Dark/Light/Catppuccin/Dracula/Nord/HighContrast)
- density_mode (Compact/Cozy/Spacious)
- focus_region (Results/Detail) → later replaced by FocusGraph (2noh9.3.16)
- input_mode (Query/Agent/Workspace/CreatedFrom/CreatedTo/PaneFilter/DetailFind)
- input_buffer
- cached_detail (ConversationView with sharded LRU cache)
- show_help, show_detail_modal, show_bulk_modal
- palette_state, match_mode (Standard/Prefix)
- ranking_mode (6 modes: RecentHeavy/Balanced/Relevance/MatchQuality/DateNewest/DateOldest)
- detail_tab (Messages/Snippets/Raw)
- context_window (S/M/L/XL)
- search_mode (Lexical/Semantic/Hybrid)
- saved_views (9 slots)
- query_history (50 entries)
- per_pane_limit, toast state, export_modal state
- animation timers, ui_metrics state

### CassMsg Enum
Covers EVERY user action and system event:
- QueryChanged, ResultsUpdated, SelectionChanged
- FilterAdded/Removed (agent, workspace, time, source)
- ThemeCycled, DensityCycled, RankingCycled
- OpenInEditor, ExportTriggered
- IndexProgress, SearchComplete
- ModalOpened/Closed, ToastShown/Dismissed
- FocusChanged, Resize, Tick
- LoadState, SaveState

### Service Boundaries
- SearchService: async search execution (Tantivy/vector queries)
- IndexService: background indexing with progress updates
- EditorService: spawn editor for result viewing
- ExportService: HTML/markdown export
- PersistenceService: save/load TUI state

### Side Effects via Cmd<CassMsg>
- Cmd::perform for async operations (search, index, export)
- Cmd::none() for pure state transitions
- Cmd::batch for grouped commands

## Implementation
Place in `src/ui/app.rs` (new file justified by genuinely new architectural pattern — Elm Model vs monolithic loop). This is the foundational type definition that all subsequent feature work builds on.

## Tests
- Unit: CassApp::new() initializes with sane defaults
- Unit: All CassMsg variants are constructible
- Unit: Service boundaries compile with correct types

## Acceptance Criteria
- CassApp has every field from current tui.rs state
- CassMsg covers every event in the current 5000-line match block
- Service interfaces defined as traits
- No orphan fields (every field used by at least one CassMsg handler)

## Depends On
- 2noh9.1.4 (FrankenTUI deep-dive — informs API usage)
- 2noh9.2.1 (ftui dependency available)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:25:45.646350547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:58:32.414935903Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T17:58:32.414898834Z","close_reason":"Completed: Created src/ui/app.rs with CassApp Model (all state fields from tui.rs), CassMsg enum (60+ variants covering every user action and system event), 5 service traits (Search, Index, Editor, Export, Persistence), PersistedState type, and supporting enums. All 11 unit tests pass, cargo check clean, no clippy issues in new code.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","ftui","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:42:14.525951153Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:25:45.646350547Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:42:17.113551376Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.3","title":"Task: Replace Ratatui event loop with ftui Program skeleton (renders + handles keys + exits cleanly)","description":"## Goal
Have cass tui run an ftui Program with a minimal but real app model: enters terminal session (raw mode, alt-screen or chosen screen mode), renders, handles keys, exits cleanly. Wires the full update() state machine.

## What (Merged from Phase 1B + 1D)

### Program Entry Point
Replace the current run_tui() function in main.rs/tui.rs with:
```rust
ftui_runtime::Program::new(CassApp::new(config))?
```

Configure ProgramConfig:
- screen_mode = ScreenMode::AltScreen (full-screen TUI like current)
- tick_rate ~16ms for 60fps
- mouse_capture = true

Wire CassApp::subscriptions() to return Every::new(tick_rate) for periodic updates (indexing progress, animation ticks).

### Delete Manual Terminal Management
This is where we delete:
- Manual crossterm::terminal::enable_raw_mode / disable_raw_mode
- EnterAlternateScreen / LeaveAlternateScreen
- Manual event::poll loop
The Program handles all of this via RAII.

### update() State Machine
Implement CassApp::update() handling every CassMsg variant. Key patterns:
1. QueryChanged → debounced search via Cmd::perform (async search then ResultsUpdated)
2. ResultsUpdated → rebuilds panes via rebuild_panes_with_filter
3. Selection/scroll changes → update state directly with Cmd::none()
4. Filter changes → update filters struct and trigger re-search
5. OpenInEditor → spawns editor via Cmd::perform
6. IndexProgress → updates the progress display state

CRITICAL: The update function must be unit-testable without a terminal — pure state transitions.

## Tests
- Unit: Program::new() creates a valid program instance
- Unit: update() handles QueryChanged → produces search Cmd
- Unit: update() handles ResultsUpdated → updates pane state correctly
- Unit: All CassMsg variants handled (no unreachable! or todo!())
- Integration: Program runs headless, receives QueryChanged, produces results
- E2E: cass launches, shows search bar, accepts input, exits cleanly on Ctrl+C

## Acceptance Criteria
- No more manual crossterm terminal management code
- ftui Program handles full lifecycle (init → render → update → exit)
- update() is a pure function (no terminal I/O in update)
- Headless mode (TUI_HEADLESS=1) still works
- Exit cleanly on Ctrl+C, panic, and signal

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.15","title":"Task: Charts + data visualization on ftui (heatmap, bar charts, sparklines, analytics views)","description":"## Why
cass has rich data (session counts, token usage, agent activity) but no way to visualize it in the TUI. ftui-extras provides charts (bar, line, area, scatter) and canvas (pixel-level drawing) modules that can bring data alive in the terminal — a unique differentiator for a coding agent search tool.

## What
Leverage ftui-extras/charts and canvas for terminal data visualization:

1. **Session activity heatmap**: Calendar-style heatmap showing coding session frequency per day (like GitHub contribution graph, but in the terminal). Accessible via command palette or dedicated Analytics tab.
2. **Agent usage bar chart**: Visual breakdown of sessions by agent (Claude vs Codex vs Gemini etc.) — horizontal bars with agent accent colors.
3. **Token usage sparkline in detail view**: Show token consumption over the conversation using Sparkline with gradient colors (green=low, yellow=medium, red=high). Benefits from z9fse analytics data when available.
4. **Search frequency timeline**: Mini chart in footer showing queries per minute (useful for debugging search performance).
5. **Indexing performance chart**: Throughput over time during reindexing.

## Implementation
- Use ftui-extras charts module: BarChart, LineChart, Sparkline
- Use ftui-extras canvas module: pixel-level drawing primitives for heatmap cells
- Data sources: daily_stats table (already computed by analytics pipeline), real-time counters for search/indexing
- Charts render as normal ftui widgets composable in any layout area
- Responsive: charts adapt to available space (min 40 cols for bar chart, 20 cols for sparkline)

## Tests
- Unit: Chart rendering with known data produces expected cells
- Snapshot: Heatmap golden test with mock data
- Unit: Sparkline handles edge cases (empty data, single point, overflow)
- Integration: Charts display real data from daily_stats

## Acceptance Criteria
- At least 3 visualizations implemented
- Charts readable on 80x24 terminal
- Agent colors consistent with theme system
- Degrades gracefully: text-only summary at EssentialOnly level

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.16","title":"Task: JSON viewer for raw message inspection on ftui (collapsible tree, syntax highlighting, copy)","description":"## Why
The TUI has a Raw tab in the detail pane, but it's just unformatted text. A proper JSON viewer is invaluable for debugging connector issues, understanding agent-specific message formats, and inspecting token usage data (extra_json/extra_bin fields). No other TUI search tool offers this.

## What
Add ftui_widgets::json_view::JsonView for inspecting raw JSON data of search results and messages:
1. **Raw message JSON** with syntax-highlighted keys/values/strings/numbers/booleans
2. **Collapsible nested objects and arrays** (click or Enter to expand/collapse)
3. **Copy-to-clipboard** for individual values (via OSC52 clipboard integration)
4. **Search within JSON** content (Ctrl+F in JSON view)
5. **Path display**: Shows JSON path (e.g., $.messages[0].usage.input_tokens) for selected node

## Implementation
- Use ftui_widgets::json_view::JsonView (tree-based collapsible JSON display)
- Wire as CassMsg::ToggleJsonView — toggle with J key in detail view or command palette
- Render conditionally in the detail pane area (replaces Raw tab content when active)
- Parse message extra_json/extra_bin into serde_json::Value for display
- Syntax colors: keys=blue, strings=green, numbers=yellow, booleans=cyan, null=gray

## Tests
- Unit: JSON parsing handles all serde_json::Value variants
- Snapshot: Render known JSON structure, verify layout and colors
- Unit: Collapse/expand toggle works correctly
- E2E: Navigate to result → press J → see JSON → press J → back to normal

## Acceptance Criteria
- Toggle between normal detail view and JSON view with single keypress
- All JSON types rendered with appropriate syntax colors
- Collapse/expand for nested structures
- Copy individual values to clipboard
- Works with all 14 connector message formats

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.17","title":"Task: HTML/SVG TUI screenshot export on ftui (capture current view as shareable file)","description":"## Why
Users want to capture beautiful screenshots of their cass TUI for sharing, documentation, or bug reports without external tools. This is different from export-html (which exports conversation content) — this exports the actual TUI visual state. ftui-extras/export renders Buffer snapshots as self-contained HTML or SVG.

## What
Leverage ftui-extras/export module for TUI-to-file export:
1. **Export as HTML**: Captures exactly what the user sees, preserving all colors and formatting as a self-contained HTML file
2. **Export as SVG**: High-quality vector output for documentation, presentations, READMEs
3. **Export as plain text**: Stripped ANSI for accessibility or plain-text contexts
4. **Auto-export on asciicast stop**: Generate an HTML thumbnail of the final frame

## Implementation
- Use ftui-extras export module: buffer_to_html(), buffer_to_svg(), buffer_to_text()
- Triggered via command palette action ("Export TUI screenshot") or keybinding
- Default output: ~/Downloads/cass_screenshot_<timestamp>.{html|svg|txt}
- File format selection via command palette sub-menu
- Toast notification on successful export with path

## Tests
- Unit: buffer_to_html() produces valid HTML for known buffer state
- Unit: buffer_to_svg() produces valid SVG with correct colors
- Snapshot: Export of mock TUI state matches golden reference
- E2E: Trigger export → verify file created with correct content

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 3 formats implemented (HTML, SVG, text)
- Colors preserved accurately in HTML/SVG output
- Files are self-contained (no external dependencies)
- Output filename includes timestamp to prevent overwrites
- Works from both command palette and keyboard shortcut

-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18","title":"Epic: FrankenTUI analytics dashboard (tokens, cost, tools, plans, projects)","description":"## Why
Token/usage analytics is the missing “quantified self” layer for cass: we already index the entire agent work history, and we already compute token usage/cost signals in SQLite, but there is no best-in-class terminal experience for exploring it.

This epic turns cass into the world’s most functional analytics TUI for coding-agent work by leveraging FrankenTUI (ftui) strengths: deterministic rendering, composable widgets, virtualization, charts/canvas, inspector overlays, and recording/replay for reproducible bug reports.

## User Stories (What This Must Enable)
- “How many tokens did I burn today / this week / this month?” (hour/day/week/month)
- “Which projects/workspaces are costing me the most?” (top-N workspaces; trends)
- “Which agents/models are the biggest spend?” (Codex vs Claude vs Gemini; model breakdown)
- “What is my burn rate right now?” (tokens/hour live + trailing averages)
- “How tool-heavy am I?” (tool calls per 1k tokens; per-tool breakdown)
- “How much planning am I doing?” (plan messages, plan token share)
- “Where is the data real vs estimated?” (API coverage, quality/coverage heatmaps)

## Data Sources (Already In Cass)
- SQLite analytics tables:
  - message-level fact tables: token_usage (v10), message_metrics (v11)
  - rollups: token_daily_stats (v10), usage_hourly + usage_daily (v11)
  - pricing seeds: model_pricing (v10)
- Connectors already extract model/tool/token metadata into NormalizedMessage.extra; analytics ingest reads it.

## IA / Views (Initial Target Set)
1. Overview dashboard: KPI tiles + mini sparklines (today, 7d, 30d)
2. Time-series explorer: interactive chart (metric + group-by + overlays)
3. Calendar heatmap: daily tokens or sessions with color legend + drilldown
4. Breakdowns: tables with sparklines for agents/workspaces/models (sortable, virtualized)
5. Tools view: per-tool usage (calls, avg tokens/call, cost/call)
6. Plans view: plan frequency + plan token share + drift over time
7. Coverage view: where we have API tokens vs estimates, by agent/model/source
8. Drilldown: selecting any bucket opens a filtered search/results view pre-scoped to that slice

## Interaction Model
- Command palette entry: “Analytics” (and sub-commands)
- Global filters: time range, agent, workspace, source, role, metric
- Drilldown is first-class: Enter on any chart/table row navigates to details
- Mouse support where it helps (hover tooltip on charts, scroll)
- Consistent key hints + discoverability via ftui hint registry

## Performance Principles
- Prefer rollup tables for all interactive views (O(#buckets) queries).
- Avoid full message scans; allow explicit “deep analysis” mode when necessary.
- Cache query results per (filters, metric, group-by) with fast invalidation on index refresh.
- Virtualize long lists/tables; render only visible rows.

## Testing & Logging Mandate
- Deterministic rendering snapshots via ftui harness (goldens).
- PTY e2e tests: drive the analytics UI, assert stable buffers, log frame timings.
- Every view emits structured debug logs when enabled (query timing, rows read, cache hits).

## Dependencies (Not Enforced At Epic Level)
Implementation tasks under this epic will depend on:
- ftui TUI foundation (2noh9.*)
- analytics CLI/query surface (z9fse.3)
- model_name + cost support (z9fse.11, z9fse.10)
- per-tool breakdown (z9fse.6)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:10:32.201778623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:10:32.201778623Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","ftui","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:10:32.201778623Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18.1","title":"Task: Spec analytics dashboard IA + interactions (views, filters, drilldown, keymap)","description":"## Goal
Write the definitive interaction spec for the ftui analytics dashboards so implementation is cohesive, fast, and consistent with cass power-user workflows.

This spec must be self-contained: future implementers should not need to re-read earlier plans to understand what the analytics UI is supposed to do.

## Inputs / Constraints
- Data is already stored in SQLite rollups (usage_hourly/usage_daily/token_daily_stats) and fact tables (message_metrics/token_usage).
- Prefer rollup-driven UI queries (O(#buckets)), avoid scanning message_metrics except in explicit “deep analysis” flows.
- All time semantics are UTC unless the UI explicitly supports per-user local time zones (out of scope for v1; document that we’re UTC).
- Must be navigable entirely by keyboard; mouse is optional enhancement.

## Information Architecture (Proposed)
Top-level: add an “Analytics” surface accessible via:
- Command palette: `Analytics: Dashboard`, `Analytics: Explorer`, `Analytics: Breakdowns`, `Analytics: Tools`, `Analytics: Cost`, `Analytics: Coverage`.
- Optional hotkey: `g a` (go-to analytics) or `Alt+A`.

Inside Analytics:
1. **Dashboard** (default)
   - KPI tiles: tokens today / 7d / 30d, cost today / 30d (if available), burn rate tokens/hr, tool calls per 1k tokens, plan token share.
   - Mini sparklines per KPI.
   - “Top movers” (agents/projects) with delta vs prior period.

2. **Explorer** (time-series)
   - Primary chart area (line/area/stacked).
   - Controls: metric selector, group-by selector, overlay selector.
   - Overlay options: by agent, by workspace, by source, by model.
   - Cursor crosshair + tooltip.

3. **Heatmap** (calendar)
   - Calendar grid with color scale.
   - Toggle between session count, api tokens, cost.
   - Drilldown from a day -> Explorer pre-filtered.

4. **Breakdowns** (tables)
   - Virtualized table with sortable columns.
   - Per-row sparkline (trend over range).
   - Dimensions: agent, workspace, source, model.

5. **Tools**
   - Per-tool breakdown: calls, avg tokens/call, avg cost/call, trend.
   - Ability to filter to a tool and jump to Search view for sessions that used it.

6. **Cost/Models**
   - Models list with stacked token breakdown + USD estimate.
   - Provider-level aggregation.

7. **Coverage**
   - Coverage heatmap: % API token data vs estimated by (agent x time).
   - Highlight sources/connectors with weak coverage.

## Global Filters (Persisted While In Analytics)
- Time range: presets (today, 7d, 30d, 90d, YTD, all) + custom since/until.
- Agent filter: multi-select.
- Workspace filter: multi-select with search.
- Source filter: local/remote/source_id.
- Role filter (optional for advanced).

These should be displayed in a “filter ribbon” and mirrored in a compact “filters drawer” for small terminals.

## Drilldown Semantics (Critical)
- Enter on any bucket (chart point / heatmap cell) -> opens the main Search results view pre-scoped:
  - since/until set to bucket range
  - agent/workspace/source filters inherited
  - query empty (user can type)

- Enter on any breakdown row -> same, plus an additional filter (e.g., agent=codex).

## Keymap (Draft)
- `Tab` / `Shift+Tab`: cycle analytics subviews
- `g`: open “go to” palette within analytics
- `/`: open filter/search within current view (e.g., table search)
- `Enter`: drilldown
- `Esc`: back (pop view stack)
- `?`: help overlay (view-specific key hints)

## Visual Style (ftui-first)
- Strong typography hierarchy: big KPIs, small labels, consistent number formatting.
- Color scales:
  - tokens: cool -> warm (blue/green -> yellow -> red)
  - cost: green -> amber -> red
  - coverage: red (bad) -> green (good)
- Always include legends for heatmaps/charts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A concrete, implementable spec describing:
  - views, layouts, and minimum terminal sizes
  - filters and drilldown behaviors
  - keymap + command palette entries
  - data semantics (UTC; week/month definitions)
- Explicitly calls out which UI surfaces depend on which analytics beads:
  - z9fse.12 query library
  - z9fse.6 tools breakdown
  - z9fse.10 cost estimation
  - z9fse.11 model_name

## Testing Notes
- Define snapshot test targets (which screens, which fixtures) so engineers know what to lock down.
- Define PTY e2e paths (launch -> open analytics -> switch view -> drilldown -> exit).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:50.160059329Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T20:31:38.608901310Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T20:31:38.608869390Z","close_reason":"Completed: analytics dashboard IA + interactions spec added to docs/tui_keymap_rfc.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","design","ftui","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:50.160059329Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18.10","title":"Task: Analytics drilldown -> Search (push filters + time range + dim; preserve back stack)","description":"## Goal
Make analytics actionable by enabling drilldown into the core cass search experience.

If a chart shows a spike, the user must be able to jump straight to the sessions/messages that caused it.

## Behavior
- Enter on any analytics selection (bucket, row, tool, model) should:
  1. push a new view on the navigation stack (Search view)
  2. pre-fill time filters (since/until) to the selected bucket
  3. apply inherited global filters (agent/workspace/source)
  4. apply the selection’s dimension filter (e.g., agent=codex or workspace=/foo)
  5. leave query empty by default (user types next)

- Esc from Search returns back to Analytics with prior selection preserved.

## Implementation Notes
- Reuse the ftui filter state system (2noh9.3.3); do not create a second filter stack.
- Ensure drilldown uses UTC boundaries consistent with analytics bucketing semantics.
- Avoid triggering double-searches on navigation (debounce or batch state changes).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Drilldown works for:
  - Explorer bucket -> Search
  - Heatmap day -> Search
  - Breakdowns row -> Search
  - Tools row -> Search (when tool filter is supported)
  - Cost/model row -> Search (model filter)
- Back navigation preserves analytics selection and scroll.

## Testing & Logging
- Unit: drilldown state transition produces correct filter state.
- PTY e2e: open analytics -> drilldown -> verify search view is filtered -> back.
- Logging: record drilldown payload (bucket + dim) in debug logs.","notes":"BrownEagle: fixed drilldown filter inheritance in src/ui/app.rs (search filters now reset from analytics filters to prevent stale leakage; selected agent dimension overrides inherited agent set).  {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.4.18.11","title":"Task: Analytics UI test suite (ftui snapshots + PTY e2e + perf logging)","description":"## Goal
Lock down end-to-end correctness of the full FrankenTUI analytics suite and prevent regressions as views, interactions, and query semantics evolve.

Analytics is especially susceptible to subtle defects (bucket boundaries, coverage math, drilldown scoping, stale caches, layout breakage). This task is the final confidence gate.

## Required Coverage Surface
The suite must cover **all analytics subviews**:
- dashboard
- explorer
- heatmap
- breakdowns
- tools
- cost/models
- coverage
- plans

## Test Layers
1. **Snapshot/golden tests (ftui-harness)**
   - each analytics subview at 80x24 and 120x40
   - representative states: normal, sparse, empty, and loading/error
   - deterministic fixtures for stable diffs

2. **Behavioral unit tests**
   - navigation stack and focus transitions across subviews
   - filter persistence and inheritance
   - deterministic sorting/ranking/tie-break behavior
   - drilldown payload construction correctness

3. **PTY e2e tests**
   - launch `cass tui --once` in headless PTY harness
   - navigate across all analytics subviews
   - execute drilldown to Search from multiple subviews and return
   - assert exit code 0, no panic output, and expected rendered states

4. **Performance guard assertions (non-flaky)**
   - capture query/render timings per view
   - enforce broad safety thresholds in CI (catch catastrophic regressions)

## Logging Requirements
On any failure, emit high-signal diagnostics:
- rendered buffer artifacts and diffs
- selected filters/time-range/context
- query timing + cache-hit trace
- drilldown payload and navigation trace when relevant

## Dependency Policy
This task is intentionally blocked on all analytics view implementation beads so it cannot be prematurely marked complete while major surfaces remain untested.","acceptance_criteria":"1. 4. Performance logs are captured and thresholded to catch severe regressions.
